meta.description: Train random forest classifiers and rank biomarker features by importance using mean decrease in accuracy and Gini impurity. Inputs: feature matrix, class labels. Outputs: trained model, importance table, OOB error curve, partial dependence plots.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims, unverifiable statistics, or invented study results appeared in any audited execution.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed inside offline statistical execution boundaries and did not issue diagnostic, treatment, or clinical decision advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow remained aligned with binary random-forest feature ranking, and the documentation clearly warns that preprocessing, imputation, and multiclass use are out of scope.

code_usability.detail: The CLI help check, packaged tests, canonical runs, plot-only rerender, stress run, and repeated seeded comparison all executed successfully in the audited environment.

functional_suitability.note: The skill fully covers its promised binary random-forest training, importance export, plot generation, plot-only reuse, troubleshooting, and test flows.

reliability.note: Validation, standardized errors, timeout control, and deterministic reruns are strong, although corrupted-bundle recovery guidance remains primarily documentation-based rather than explicitly smoke-tested.

performance_context.note: Full score achieved. The skill uses progressive disclosure and a direct CLI workflow without unnecessary context or execution overhead.

agent_usability.note: The structure is clear and consistent, but first-use onboarding still benefits from scanning a long option matrix before reaching the most common execution scenarios.

human_usability.note: The trigger language and examples are natural for technical users, but the skill remains intentionally strict about already-cleaned binary inputs.

security.note: Full score achieved. Output paths are constrained to the skill root, inputs are validated, execution is offline-only, and the workflow does not expose eval-like code execution.

maintainability.note: Full score achieved. The codebase is modular, references are separated cleanly, and the packaged tests verify both full-run and plot-only behavior.

agent_specific.note: Full score achieved. Trigger precision, layered references, composability, deterministic re-runs, and explicit out-of-scope boundaries are all strong.

inputs.label: Bundled dataset full analysis
inputs.note: Executed cleanly and produced the full documented artifact set, including session metadata, model bundle, ranked tables, and both plots.

assertions.text: Output directory contains the documented artifact set.
assertions.note: session_info.txt, data/rf_result.rds, both CSV tables, and both PDF plots were present.

assertions.text: rf_top_features.csv honors the requested reporting cap.
assertions.note: The run used --rf_top_n 5 and the output contained exactly 5 feature rows.

assertions.text: Reproducibility metadata is recorded.
assertions.note: session_info.txt was written successfully for the canonical run.

assertions.text: The run stays within local-file execution boundaries.
assertions.note: No network access or unsafe command execution was observed.

inputs.label: Custom importance metric and thresholds
inputs.note: Handled a tuned forest and Gini-based ranking without instability, and the filtered output remained coherent and within the requested cap.

assertions.text: Custom modeling parameters execute successfully on the bundled data.
assertions.note: The CLI completed with --rf_ntree 800, --rf_mtry 4, and --rf_imp_type 2.

assertions.text: The importance metric switches to MeanDecreaseGini when requested.
assertions.note: rf_feature_importance.csv reported MeanDecreaseGini in the Metric column.

assertions.text: The filtered feature table honors the requested threshold and cap.
assertions.note: The filtered table retained 7 rows, which is within the requested cap of 8, and all retained scores exceeded 1.

assertions.text: Plot generation remains stable under custom settings.
assertions.note: Both PDF outputs were created successfully in the custom output directory.

inputs.label: Identical case and control labels
inputs.note: The expected validation failure was returned immediately with a standardized message, which is correct behavior for this boundary case.

assertions.text: The CLI rejects identical case and control labels.
assertions.note: The command returned SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER: --case_group and --control_group must be different.

assertions.text: Validation happens before model training or result writing.
assertions.note: No training, plotting, or output-writing log lines appeared before the error.

assertions.text: The failure message is standardized and actionable.
assertions.note: The error uses a documented skill-specific code and clearly identifies the invalid setting.

assertions.text: Boundary handling remains deterministic and safe.
assertions.note: The same invalid request is expected to fail consistently without side effects.

inputs.label: Plot-only rerender from existing model
inputs.note: The plot-only branch reused the saved model bundle and regenerated plots without retraining.

assertions.text: Plot-only mode succeeds without raw input-file arguments.
assertions.note: The rerun completed with only plot-only, output, seed, and timeout parameters.

assertions.text: Plot-only mode reuses the existing model bundle from output_dir/data/rf_result.rds.
assertions.note: The command succeeded against the existing canonical output directory whose saved model bundle was already present.

assertions.text: Plot outputs exist after the rerender.
assertions.note: Both plot/rf_error_plot.pdf and plot/rf_importance_plot.pdf were present after the rerun.

assertions.text: Plot-only mode avoids unnecessary retraining side effects.
assertions.note: The log showed startup and plotting only, with no repeated input-read or training messages.

inputs.label: Heavier forest on bundled data
inputs.note: A heavier forest configuration completed within the timeout and still produced an interpretable ranked table.

assertions.text: The heavier random-forest configuration completes successfully within the configured timeout.
assertions.note: The run with --rf_ntree 1200, --rf_mtry 6, and --rf_nodesize 1 completed successfully under the 300-second limit.

assertions.text: The thresholded top-feature table honors the requested cap and threshold.
assertions.note: The stress output contained 10 rows, matching the cap, and all retained importance values exceeded 0.5.

assertions.text: The ranked output remains interpretable under the heavier settings.
assertions.note: The ranking remained coherent, led by SMAD7 and KLF2 rather than collapsing into unusable output.

assertions.text: Stress execution preserves the documented artifact contract.
assertions.note: Model bundle, tables, plots, and session metadata were all generated successfully.

key_strengths: The skill is operationally stable across standard, tuned, plot-only, stress, and expected-failure workflows.
Input validation, path confinement, and offline-only execution give the CLI a strong safety profile.
Deterministic behavior is explicit and was confirmed by a repeated seeded run with byte-for-byte matching output.
Documentation, examples, troubleshooting, and packaged tests align closely with the actual implementation.

recommendations.title: Add a faster quick-start entry
recommendations.problem: The skill is complete, but first-time users and agents still need to scan a long option table before reaching the most common execution scenarios.
recommendations.root_cause: The documentation currently optimizes for completeness before the shortest successful path.
recommendations.fix: Add a short Quick Start section near the top of SKILL.md with three copy-ready commands: standard run, tuned run, and plot-only rerender. Keep the full option matrix below for reference depth.

recommendations.title: Expand negative smoke-test coverage
recommendations.problem: The audited package tests cover full execution and plot-only reuse, but they do not directly exercise path-escape rejection or corrupted model-bundle handling.
recommendations.root_cause: The current test suite focuses on the main happy path plus one plot-only regression path.
recommendations.fix: Add one path-safety test for an output directory outside the skill root and one plot-only test with an intentionally broken rf_result.rds bundle to verify the documented failure contract.
